'''Jurahi''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the last decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A member of the [[Almas Council]], a satellite [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] which oversaw Jedi business in the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]], Jurahi held the title [[Master of Visions]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Jurahi was [[Jedi training/Legends|educated]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], eventually attaining the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. A pacifist, Master Jurahi had trained as a [[Jedi Consular|Consular]], specializing as a [[Jedi Seer|Seer]]. Stationed in the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]], Master Jurahi was appointed as a [[Jedi instructor|instructor]] at the [[Almas Academy]], an experimental [[Jedi academy|academy]] on the planet [[Almas]]. Master Jurahi earned the title of [[Master of Visions]] at the Academy and was made a member of the [[Almas Council]] by the time of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]].<ref name="CAM" />

As a member of the Council and a leading administrator, Master Jurahi was present when Master [[Darrus Jeht/Legends|Darrus Jeht]] arrived from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] baring orders from the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. To Jurahi's dismay, all excess personnel at the academy were to report to staging areas throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to gain membership in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. Already a man who disdained fighting and viewed [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who "did their thinking with their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]]" as the greatest threat to the galaxy, Jurahi was mortified by the demand. He quietly resigned himself to the unchangeable demands, although he did at least give in to irritation long enough to complain, "Who does the Council think we are? We are a school, not a garrison!"<ref name="CAM" />
